title: Replace explicit-directional SF Symbols with auto-mirroring variants
summary: Replaced 3 non-auto-mirroring SF Symbol call sites with auto-mirroring variants. chevron.right -> chevron.forward in TodayView:566; arrow.right.circle -> arrow.forward.circle in WriteView:88 and :181. xcodebuild build verified. Moved HIG-AR-001 + HIG-AR-002 from open to in-progress with source_fix_confirmed=true in both the RTL gate manifest and the all-locale HIG matrix. Closure requires post-fix Arabic screenshot evidence under app-localization-hig-multisurface-screenshot-capture.

source_changes.closes_finding: HIG-AR-001 (source fix; awaiting screenshot rerun for full closure)

not_claimed: HIG-AR-001 or HIG-AR-002 is fully closed (requires post-fix screenshot evidence)
RTL mirroring is proven on Owlory localized surfaces
residual_risk: HIG-AR-001 and HIG-AR-002 remain in-progress until a post-fix Arabic screenshot is preserved under automation/proofs/.

Apple HIG only documents .right/.left vs .forward/.backward for the SF Symbols family; if Owlory later adds new explicit-directional SF Symbols (e.g., from a different glyph family), the same auto-mirror rule still needs to be applied.

RTL audit covered chevron / arrow only; if other direction-bearing icons exist that I missed in the source grep (e.g., custom drawn glyphs, asset-catalog images, decorative artwork), they are NOT covered by this fix.
downstream_unblocked_slices: app-localization-hig-multisurface-screenshot-capture (pri 78; can now run with the fixed RTL state captured)
